best_for: building the field map before reading primary papers

subfields.name: 🧭 Post-training surveys
subfields.focus: field-level maps of post-training, reasoning models, and data-centric LLM practice
subfields.key_risk: survey taxonomy hides concrete data objects

subfields.name: 🧠 Reasoning LLM surveys
subfields.focus: reasoning model lineages, claims, and recurring evaluation patterns
subfields.key_risk: model-centric framing obscures data and verifier details

subfields.name: 📦 Data documentation / datasheets
subfields.focus: datasheets, data statements, lineage, license, and release metadata
subfields.key_risk: reusable data lacks provenance or consent context

subfields.name: 🧪 RLHF / reward-model surveys
subfields.focus: background needed to connect preference data, reward models, and reasoning rewards
subfields.key_risk: generic alignment lessons are over-applied to verifiable reasoning

subfields.name: 🌐 Agent data / tool-use surveys
subfields.focus: orientation for tools, web tasks, OS tasks, and repository agents
subfields.key_risk: agent traces are treated as transcripts rather than replayable episodes

subfields.name: 🧯 Contamination / evaluation surveys
subfields.focus: reproducibility, contamination, model collapse, and benchmark refresh
subfields.key_risk: benchmark deltas are accepted without data-overlap checks

best_for: understanding where reasoning-data objects came from

subfields.name: 🧱 Instruction tuning / SFT data
subfields.focus: demonstrations, instruction mixtures, and target-answer records
subfields.key_risk: prompt sources and mixture weights are undisclosed

subfields.name: 🤝 Human preference data / RLHF
subfields.focus: comparisons, scalar rewards, reward models, and human feedback pipelines
subfields.key_risk: annotator or reward-model assumptions are hidden

subfields.name: ⚖️ DPO / preference optimization
subfields.focus: direct preference learning and pairwise data as policy supervision
subfields.key_risk: preference pairs are reused outside their collection context

subfields.name: 🧠 Chain-of-thought / rationale data
subfields.focus: rationales, traces, self-consistency, and reasoning-style supervision
subfields.key_risk: trace style is mistaken for faithful reasoning

subfields.name: 🔁 Self-training / STaR / Self-Instruct
subfields.focus: self-generated instructions, bootstrapped traces, critique loops, and filtering
subfields.key_risk: generated data collapses diversity or repeats teacher artifacts

subfields.name: 🤖 RLAIF / synthetic feedback
subfields.focus: model-generated preferences, critiques, and constitutional feedback
subfields.key_risk: synthetic feedback encodes hidden judge behavior

best_for: RLVR, answer-verifiable data, and executable reasoning tasks

subfields.name: 📐 Math answer-verifiable data
subfields.focus: math problems, final answers, solution traces, and answer checkers
subfields.key_risk: answer extraction and normalization inflate scores

subfields.name: 🧮 Math RLVR datasets
subfields.focus: math records used for rejection sampling, SFT, PRMs, and RLVR
subfields.key_risk: data reuse and contamination are not reported

subfields.name: 💻 Code execution / unit-test data
subfields.focus: code problems, unit tests, generated tests, execution logs, and repair tasks
subfields.key_risk: flaky or leaked tests become the reward

subfields.name: 🧾 Formal proof / Lean / theorem proving
subfields.focus: Lean, proof scripts, tactic environments, theorem statements, and proof checkers
subfields.key_risk: proof succeeds only under an undocumented environment

subfields.name: 🧪 Verifier robustness and answer extraction
subfields.focus: false positives, false negatives, checker brittleness, and adversarial formats
subfields.key_risk: model learns verifier quirks instead of task skill

subfields.name: 🧰 Programmatic benchmarks
subfields.focus: evaluation sets whose scoring can become a post-training signal
subfields.key_risk: benchmark scoring is treated as a reusable training verifier without audit

best_for: step-level rewards, PRM research, and verifier-model design

subfields.name: 🪜 Human step-level labels
subfields.focus: human-labeled intermediate steps and first-error annotations
subfields.key_risk: step boundaries and label policy are ambiguous

subfields.name: 🧪 Process reward models
subfields.focus: PRMs, process verifiers, calibration, and reward-model training
subfields.key_risk: process reward rises while final correctness does not

subfields.name: 🔁 Rollout-value supervision
subfields.focus: rollout values, search-derived labels, and automated progress signals
subfields.key_risk: rollout policy leaks solver strength into labels

subfields.name: 🛠️ Automatic process supervision
subfields.focus: programmatic or model-generated process labels without dense human annotation
subfields.key_risk: automatic labels silently inherit verifier bias

subfields.name: ❌ First-error localization
subfields.focus: where a solution first becomes invalid and how that signal is used
subfields.key_risk: localized errors are not causally linked to final correction

subfields.name: 📊 PRM benchmarks and evaluation
subfields.focus: ProcessBench, PRMBench, Qwen PRM, and evaluation surfaces for process rewards
subfields.key_risk: PRM benchmark success does not transfer to training-time use

best_for: tool, web, OS, app, and repository-level trajectory data

subfields.name: 🛠️ Tool-use data
subfields.focus: tool calls, function signatures, API banks, and tool-use traces
subfields.key_risk: tool schemas change or hide execution failures

subfields.name: 🌍 Web/browser agents
subfields.focus: web tasks, browser state, navigation traces, and page observations
subfields.key_risk: web state is not replayable after collection

subfields.name: 📱 App/mobile agents
subfields.focus: mobile apps, app-world tasks, UI actions, and user simulators
subfields.key_risk: UI state and app versions are not pinned

subfields.name: 🖥️ OS/desktop agents
subfields.focus: desktop/OS tasks, filesystem state, shell actions, and multi-app workflows
subfields.key_risk: hidden environment state makes episodes non-reproducible

subfields.name: 🧑‍💻 SWE/repository agents
subfields.focus: GitHub issues, code patches, tests, commits, and repository repair episodes
subfields.key_risk: repository commit, tests, and scaffold are not pinned

subfields.name: 🔁 Replayable trajectory data
subfields.focus: state-action-observation schemas, terminal predicates, and failure traces
subfields.key_risk: success transcript cannot be replayed or audited

subfields.name: 🧰 Agent benchmarks and terminal predicates
subfields.focus: agent evaluation suites, task resets, terminal predicates, and success/failure labels
subfields.key_risk: benchmark score is reported without a replayable terminal predicate

best_for: rubrics, LLM judges, high-stakes domains, and expert evaluation

subfields.name: ⚖️ LLM-as-judge data
subfields.focus: model judges, preference judgments, judge prompts, and evaluator models
subfields.key_risk: judge is sensitive to style, position, or prompt attacks

subfields.name: 🧑‍⚖️ Human/expert judgment
subfields.focus: human labels, expert adjudication, disagreement handling, and rubric design
subfields.key_risk: expertise and adjudication policy are not disclosed

subfields.name: 🩺 Medical reasoning / health rubrics
subfields.focus: health, biomedical, scientific, and evidence-grounded reasoning tasks
subfields.key_risk: rubrics are not calibrated for high-stakes error

subfields.name: 🛡️ Safety reasoning data
subfields.focus: safety reasoning, refusals, jailbreaks, harmfulness, and guardrail data
subfields.key_risk: safe-looking refusals replace correct domain reasoning

subfields.name: 🧾 Factuality / grounding
subfields.focus: claims, citations, retrieval grounding, fact checking, and evidence quality
subfields.key_risk: citation style masks unsupported claims

subfields.name: ⚖️ Legal reasoning
subfields.focus: legal QA, statutes, case reasoning, contracts, and expert legal rubrics
subfields.key_risk: legal splits leak templates, memorized cases, or jurisdiction-specific assumptions

subfields.name: 🏦 Financial reasoning
subfields.focus: financial QA, tabular/text numerical reasoning, filings, and analyst-style judgments
subfields.key_risk: financial splits leak templates, reports, or memorized company facts

subfields.name: 🧪 Rubric reward models
subfields.focus: rubrics as trainable rewards and domain-conditioned reward models
subfields.key_risk: rubric scores are optimized without semantic robustness

best_for: building, filtering, releasing, and reproducing reasoning datasets

subfields.name: 🧱 Prompt sourcing
subfields.focus: question pools, seed sources, licenses, difficulty, and base-model pass rates
subfields.key_risk: prompt sources are mixed without attribution or deduplication

subfields.name: ✍️ Teacher trace generation
subfields.focus: teacher models, trace policies, sampling settings, and distillation targets
subfields.key_risk: teacher identity or sampling protocol is hidden

subfields.name: 🔎 Rejection sampling / search-generated data
subfields.focus: candidate generation, search budget, filtering, and accepted/rejected examples
subfields.key_risk: only accepted traces are released, hiding verifier behavior

subfields.name: 🔁 Self-play / self-improvement
subfields.focus: self-improvement, co-evolution, generator-verifier cycles, and curricula
subfields.key_risk: feedback loop amplifies hidden biases or shortcuts

subfields.name: 🧪 Filtering and verifier refresh
subfields.focus: answer filters, judge filters, decontamination, and verifier updates
subfields.key_risk: filter thresholds become undocumented training objectives

subfields.name: 🏗️ Open reasoning data releases
subfields.focus: open datasets, code, HF releases, recipes, ablations, and reproducibility
subfields.key_risk: headline dataset is open but recipe details are not

subfields.name: 🧬 Data lineage and release metadata
subfields.focus: datasheets, splits, lineage, licensing, versioning, and known failure modes
subfields.key_risk: downstream reuse loses the release context

best_for: reading model reports as partial data disclosures

subfields.name: 🚀 DeepSeek-R1 family
subfields.focus: RLVR, distillation, reasoning traces, and public recipe disclosure
subfields.key_risk: public report describes outcomes but not enough data partitions

subfields.name: 🌙 Kimi reasoning reports
subfields.focus: long-context reasoning, RL compute, and frontier inference budgets
subfields.key_risk: test-time compute is mixed with training-data effects

subfields.name: 🐉 Qwen reasoning/math/code reports
subfields.focus: math, code, PRM, and open-weight reasoning model families
subfields.key_risk: release cards do not separate SFT, RLVR, and evaluation data

subfields.name: 🧠 Magistral / Phi / Nemotron style reports
subfields.focus: open-weight reasoning reports with partial data and reward disclosures
subfields.key_risk: model-card claims cannot be mapped to concrete data objects

subfields.name: 🧪 RLVR recipe reports
subfields.focus: reports that expose reward contracts, rollout policies, or RL scaffolds
subfields.key_risk: RL gains are attributed without verifier coverage

subfields.name: 🧬 What is disclosed vs hidden
subfields.focus: how reports reveal or hide data sources, filters, lineage, and safety mixtures
subfields.key_risk: opaque mixtures are reused as if they were open recipes

best_for: interpreting RLVR, data scaling, and inference-budget claims

subfields.name: 📈 Data scaling
subfields.focus: how number, diversity, and difficulty of examples affect reasoning performance
subfields.key_risk: unique examples and repeated rollouts are conflated

subfields.name: 🔁 Data reuse and uniqueness
subfields.focus: reuse counts, deduplication, repeated prompts, and train/test overlap
subfields.key_risk: same source examples are counted as fresh supervision

subfields.name: ⏱️ Test-time compute
subfields.focus: sampling, search, self-critique, thinking budgets, and inference-time scaling
subfields.key_risk: performance is compared under different inference budgets

subfields.name: 🎲 pass@k / sampling budget
subfields.focus: pass@k, pass@(k,T), repeated sampling, and budget-aware evaluation
subfields.key_risk: reported gains hide selection or budget changes

subfields.name: 🧪 Verifier scaling
subfields.focus: how verifier strength, refresh, and coverage scale with training
subfields.key_risk: the verifier becomes stale or easy to exploit

subfields.name: 🏋️ RLVR optimization scaling
subfields.focus: policy optimization, reward contracts, curriculum, and rollout policy
subfields.key_risk: optimizer/scaffold improvements are mistaken for data improvements

subfields.name: 🔍 Scaling attribution
subfields.focus: separating data, verifier, optimizer, model, and inference-budget effects
subfields.key_risk: ablation tables do not isolate the source of improvement

best_for: auditing leakage, contamination, verifier gaming, and judge attacks

subfields.name: 🧯 Benchmark contamination
subfields.focus: train/test overlap, stale evaluations, and benchmark refresh
subfields.key_risk: memorized items are reported as reasoning progress

subfields.name: 🔍 Search-time contamination
subfields.focus: contamination introduced by search, tools, retrieval, or inference scaffolds
subfields.key_risk: test-time tool access leaks answer traces

subfields.name: 🧬 Hidden lineage / teacher leakage
subfields.focus: teacher-model traces, synthetic data inheritance, and hidden trait transfer
subfields.key_risk: student behavior inherits undisclosed teacher artifacts

subfields.name: 🎮 Reward hacking
subfields.focus: ways reward models, tests, or judges can be optimized as shortcuts
subfields.key_risk: reward rises while real task quality falls

subfields.name: 🧪 Verifier gaming
subfields.focus: models exploiting checkers, answer formats, or judge blind spots
subfields.key_risk: verifier-passing examples are semantically wrong

subfields.name: ⚖️ LLM-as-judge attacks
subfields.focus: one-token attacks, position bias, verbosity bias, and prompt attacks
subfields.key_risk: judge score changes for non-semantic reasons

subfields.name: 🧨 Spurious rewards
subfields.focus: shortcut rewards, memorization-triggered rewards, and reward signals that correlate with the wrong behavior
subfields.key_risk: the reward signal improves while the model learns a shortcut rather than reasoning

subfields.name: 📉 Reproducibility failures
subfields.focus: decoding, evaluation, scaffold, and data reporting failures
subfields.key_risk: reported gains disappear under controlled reruns

best_for: choosing evaluation surfaces and reusable feedback contracts

subfields.name: 📐 Math benchmarks
subfields.focus: math problem sets, answer extraction, verifier compatibility, and difficulty
subfields.key_risk: short-answer normalization hides reasoning errors

subfields.name: 💻 Code benchmarks
subfields.focus: coding tasks, generated tests, hidden tests, repair tasks, and live coding
subfields.key_risk: unit tests are brittle, leaked, or too narrow

subfields.name: 🧾 Proof benchmarks
subfields.focus: formal proof datasets, proof assistants, theorem statements, and checking
subfields.key_risk: proof checker version and imports are not pinned

subfields.name: 🌐 Agent benchmarks
subfields.focus: web, tool, OS, app, and SWE environments with terminal predicates
subfields.key_risk: benchmark episodes cannot be replayed

subfields.name: ⚖️ Rubric/domain benchmarks
subfields.focus: medical, safety, legal, finance, science, factuality, and expert rubrics
subfields.key_risk: rubric or judge expertise is insufficiently disclosed

subfields.name: 🧪 Reward-model benchmarks
subfields.focus: reward model, LLM-judge, PRM, and rubric evaluation suites
subfields.key_risk: benchmark reward preference does not reflect downstream training value

subfields.name: 🧯 Live / contamination-resistant benchmarks
subfields.focus: live, refreshed, hidden, or contamination-aware evaluation
subfields.key_risk: static benchmark becomes a training target
